initial_program:
% Determine the number of rows (N) in the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.

% Derive the index of the last row (M = N1)
maxRowMinusOne(M) :- n_rows(N), M = N - 1.

% Dummy predicates to bind variables R, C and the derived index M
row(R) :- true, R >= 0, R < 100.
col(C) :- true, C >= 0, C < 100.

% Mapping each nonzero input cell to its rotated position
rotated(Rc, Cc, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0,
                           maxRowMinusOne(M),
                           Rc = C,
                           Cc = M - R,
                           row(Rc),
                           col(Cc).

% The cells (Rc,Cc) that are the image of a rotation
is_target(Rc, Cc) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, _).

% Cells that lie inside the bounding square
inside(R, C) :- maxRowMinusOne(M), R <= M, C <= M.

% Allowed colour for each output cell
allowed(Rc, Cc, Color) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, Color).

% Zeros that stay zero (outside the square)  colour 0
allowed(R, C, 0) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), not inside(R, C), color(0).

% Zeros that become 4 (inside the square but not a target)  colour 4
allowed(R, C, 4) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), inside(R, C), color(4).

% Output takes the allowed colour
output(R, C, Color) :- allowed(R, C, Color).

% Each cell must have exactly one colour
output_cell(R, C) :- true.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Zero cells fact
zero(R, C) :- input(R, C, 0).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows (N) in the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.

% Derive the index of the last row (M = N1)
maxRowMinusOne(M) :- n_rows(N), M = N - 1.

% Dummy predicates to bind variables R, C and the derived index M
row(R) :- true, R >= 0, R < 100.
col(C) :- true, C >= 0, C < 100.

% Mapping each nonzero input cell to its rotated position
rotated(Rc, Cc, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0,
                           maxRowMinusOne(M),
                           Rc = C,
                           Cc = M - R,
                           row(Rc),
                           col(Cc).

% The cells (Rc,Cc) that are the image of a rotation
is_target(Rc, Cc) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, _).

% Cells that lie inside the bounding square
inside(R, C) :- row(R), col(C), maxRowMinusOne(M), R <= M, C <= M.

% Allowed colour for each output cell
allowed(Rc, Cc, Color) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, Color).

% Zeros that stay zero (outside the square)  colour 0
allowed(R, C, 0) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), not inside(R, C), color(0).

% Zeros that become 4 (inside the square but not a target)  colour 4
allowed(R, C, 4) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), inside(R, C), color(4).

% Output takes the allowed colour
output(R, C, Color) :- allowed(R, C, Color).

% Each cell must have exactly one colour
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Zero cells fact
zero(R, C) :- input(R, C, 0).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Determine the number of rows (N) in the input grid
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _) }.

% Derive the index of the last row (M = N1)
maxRowMinusOne(M) :- n_rows(N), M = N - 1.

% Dummy predicates to bind variables R, C and the derived index M
row(R) :- true, R >= 0, R < 100.
col(C) :- true, C >= 0, C < 100.

% Mapping each nonzero input cell to its rotated position
rotated(Rc, Cc, Color) :- input(R, C, Color), Color != 0,
                           maxRowMinusOne(M),
                           Rc = C,
                           Cc = M - R,
                           row(Rc),
                           col(Cc).

% The cells (Rc,Cc) that are the image of a rotation
is_target(Rc, Cc) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, _).

% Cells that lie inside the bounding square
inside(R, C) :- row(R), col(C), maxRowMinusOne(M), R <= M, C <= M.

% Allowed colour for each output cell
allowed(Rc, Cc, Color) :- rotated(Rc, Cc, Color).

% Zeros that stay zero (outside the square)  colour 0
allowed(R, C, 0) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), not inside(R, C), color(0).

% Zeros that become 4 (inside the square but not a target)  colour 4
allowed(R, C, 4) :- zero(R, C), not is_target(R, C), inside(R, C), color(4).

% Output takes the allowed colour
output(R, C, Color) :- allowed(R, C, Color).

% Each cell must have exactly one colour
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Zero cells fact
zero(R, C) :- input(R, C, 0).

#show output/3.
